page: Microsoft Azure L4s_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, configured with 2 physical cores and 4 vCPUs with 2 threads per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB per core, and includes 960 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2158.0 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 31.48 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics show relative limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is 3990.37, and the uncached read-only memory bandwidth for large blocks is 36.35 GB/sec, both falling into the lower performance tiers. Database operations via Passmark score at 2891.14, while Redis SET operations achieve 5.68 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L4s_v4 offers a high resource density of memory and local storage relative to its 4 vCPUs, making it a viable option for workloads requiring localized data caching without the overhead of larger compute instances. The hardware is optimized for workloads such as database operations, text processing, and static web serving. It is less suited for multi-threaded compilation or medium-to-large language model inference due to lower multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth constraints.
description: Microsoft Azure L4s_v4 is an x86_64 server instance in the Lsv4 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of Premium Storage-capable local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance lacks a GPU but boasts advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX. Benchmarks reveal strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2158.0 and a single-threaded gzip compression rate of 31.48 GB/sec. Conversely, multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth are relatively weak. This resource profile delivers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and storage-centric tasks. It is best suited for database operations, text processing, and static web serving, while being less optimal for heavy multi-threaded compilation or complex LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L4s_v4 is an x86_64 server featuring a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 960 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for database and storage-centric workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L4s_v4 is an x86_64 server with 4 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 960 GB local storage, optimized for database and text processing workloads.
tagline: A storage-capable virtual server featuring high single-core clock speeds, dedicated memory allocation, and fast local NVMe storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz with 4 vCPUs and 32 GB RAM
960 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong single-core performance with a Geekbench score of 2158.0
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
Well-suited for database operations, text processing, and static web serving
Limited multi-core scaling and memory read bandwidth
